helpOutput:
Usage: opencli devto top [options]

[read] Top DEV.to articles of the day

Options:
  --limit [value]     Number of articles (default: "20")
  -f, --format <fmt>  Output format: table, plain, json, yaml, md, csv (default:
                      "table")
  --trace <mode>      Trace capture: off, on, retain-on-failure (default: "off")
  -v, --verbose       Debug output (default: false)
  -h, --help          display help for command

Access: read | Browser: no | Domain: dev.to
Example: opencli devto top -f yaml
Output columns: rank, id, title, author, reactions, comments, reading_time, published_at, tags, url

payload.title: What Even Is AI? (I Took a Break & Had to Relearn Everything)
payload.author: rohini_gaonkar

payload.title: I Didn’t Stop Building. I Just Left My Laptop.
payload.author: itsugo

payload.title: 🫡We'll Support You with All Our Might (AI Avatar v7: Pose Capture and More (VS Code and Chrome Extension))
payload.author: webdeveloperhyper

payload.title: Blue Ridge Ruby 2026: A Conference About the Long Game
payload.author: cseeman

helpOutput:
Usage: opencli devto tag [options] <tag>

[read] Latest DEV.to articles for a specific tag

Arguments:
  tag                 Tag name (e.g. javascript, python, webdev)

Options:
  --limit [value]     Number of articles (default: "20")
  -f, --format <fmt>  Output format: table, plain, json, yaml, md, csv (default:
                      "table")
  --trace <mode>      Trace capture: off, on, retain-on-failure (default: "off")
  -v, --verbose       Debug output (default: false)
  -h, --help          display help for command

Access: read | Browser: no | Domain: dev.to
Example: opencli devto tag <tag> -f yaml
Output columns: rank, id, title, author, reactions, comments, reading_time, published_at, tags, url
